events.location: Castle Rock State Park - Castle Rock Trail Camp
events.description: Philmont Training Backpacking 2. Philmont participants only. You don’t just wake up and backpack 60+ miles in 10 days so scouts and adults must participate in most (90%) of the Troop high adventure conditioning, prep and planning... 1. Pack personal gear as if you're headed to Philmont (yes, include rain gear)2. Bring a lunch in your pack\; dinner/breakfast will be Philmont-style3. Meet at Menlo Church at 8:00, we have 2 adults driving which is plenty for our 8-member crew4. Caravan to Castle Rock State Park (located on Highway 35 about 2.5 mi southeast of the junction with Highway 9)5. Hike out to reach the Castle Rock Trail Camp, about 3 miles out. This is a dry camp, so----like this weekend----we'll carry water6. We'll set up camp, then a short day-hike along the Travertine Springs trail (probably less than 5 miles)7. On Sunday morning, break camp and hike 3 miles back to the carsWe will treat this outing as if we were at Philmont:Scouts will uphold their responsibilities according to their designated role (assigned by Philmont Crew Leader)Meals will be based off the Philmont Scout Ranch 2024 Trail Food MenuWorking to establish hiking/camp routines, enhance skills, and and foster good crew dynamics in preparation for the real deal. Reference the PSR Shakedown Guides below for more information... Philmont Shakedown Guide Pt 1Philmont Shakedown Guide Pt 2Philmont 2025 Guidebook to AdventureThe Castle Rock trail camp parking lot is located 2.5 miles south of the junction of Highway 9 and Highway 35. Park inside the gate. Do not park along Highway 9 or Skyline Boulevard, or at the Saratoga Gap parking lot, in county park parking lots, or in Open Space Preserve parking lots.3 vehicles may be needed for our crew. We have only 2 parking passes, so the third vehicle will need to bring $10 cash for parking fees. Pay for extra vehicles at the entrance station. If the entrance station is closed, use the self-registration station to pay for extra vehicle fees. Bring cash! Follow posted self-registration instructions. Restrooms are located at the trailhead. Bring potable water with you, as there is no faucet near the parking lot, and water service at Castle Rock trail camp is currently NOT OPERATIONAL.

Recommend contacting Mr. Fichou about this excellent program. National Youth Leadership Training (NYLT)An action-packed program provides youth with leadership skills and experiences they can use in Scouting and are applicable in their everyday lives.Key concepts focus on what a leader must BE, what he/she must KNOW, and what he/she must DO, and are taught with a clear focus on HOW TO. Hello Camp Wente 2025 families - I conducted an orientation meeting via zoom on Thursday, June 26 at 7pm. Here is the link to the folder with the recording: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/16_rcI8oiNVArVysN5SnOKF4M9Eq-JAkW?usp=sharingAlthough I covered this (and more), I provide the following information as a reference:We arrive at camp on Sunday afternoon - at this time, we conduct a medical check, where the camp goes over the medical forms. We then have a tour of camp. We will drop off our gear at our campsite for this tour. When we get back, we will assign platform tents based on patrols. Scouts are responsible for keeping their gear tidy in their tents and the troop can get awards for taking care of our campsite. We eat at assigned tables in the dining hall. Patrol leaders will create a duty roster during the week, which is primarily waiter duty, fire warden and other tasks. There is normally a campfire presented by the camp staff Sunday evening. On Monday, campers will begin to attend merit badge sessions and we help them to locate where the sessions are. Each scout will have  a copy of their schedule, and the troop leaders have master copies. The camp can be flexible about shifts, but we try to manage this to minimize the changes. There are three sessions in the morning and three in the afternoon. Some merit badge sessions are longer than one session. The scouts should be aware of this. Lunch break is 2 hours, which gives scouts time to eat, chill and prepare for the afternoon sessions. Scouts will benefit by having a day pack to ensure they have their materials and a WATER BOTTLE. We prioritize reminding scouts to drink. Beginning Monday night, there are activities - either tot'n chip, fireman chip, a few merit badges, etc. The troop will likely have a troop event one night during the week that the Senior Patrol Leader will choose in consultation with the Patrol Leaders.There is usually an in camp campfire, but this depends on fire safety conditions.Tuesday through Thursday follows the same MB session schedule. We will work in at least one service project of at least an hour. There is free time, but scouts must think about this as the week goes fast. Some scouts visit the trading post when it is open. They can get snacks or supplies or souvenirs. We remind scouts to NOT leave food inside of their tents as wildlife can and will visit to get themselves a snack. We can store items for scouts overnight.Friday is generally a makeup day for missing MB material. In the afternoon we have campwide games - this is a fun event which allows the senior scouts to collaborate to manage troop or patrol teams in the competition. They will coordinate with scouts to determine skill  sets. Friday night, we reconcile scout MB completion. We will have met with scouts to determine their status on merit badges, so there are no surprises. There will be a closing campfire, and this one usually features troop skits, etc. We begin to pack up Friday afternoon and evening to minimize packing time in the morning on Saturday. Early on Saturday, we complete packing, clear the tents, clear the campsite and begin to load cars for departure back home. Some cars will drop scouts off, others may ask for parents to meet them at MPC. We try to group scouts so that drop-off is convenient.2025_Wente_Weekly_Schedule.pdf
A general timeline of the physical preparation and other obligations, which will be refined as we get closer to the adventure, is here.

events.summary: HIking Merit Badge 10 Mile Hike @ Lake Chabot
events.description: Hiking Merit Badge – Lake Chabot 10 MilesMark your calendars!  Below are details for our 1st 10-mile hike for the Hiking Merit Badge.  A few quick reminders:**Send me your written hiking plan BEFORE the hike. The template is attached.   hikingtripplan.pdf**If you are not able to attend THIS hike on THIS day, that is OK. You can use the same info below to plan the hike on another day that works for your family and hopefully other scout friends.  **If you want to do another/different 10-mile hike contact me to discuss your plan.**If you do the Lake Chabot hike on a different day, please take 4 pictures of yourself and the group you are hiking with at different points along the trail.  Include the lake in the background when possible.    **If you have not already signed up for hiking merit badge and you would like to, please email Mrs. Todd (kathrynt@gmail.com) and copy me (atkins.kerri@icloud.com).** ** ** ** ** **Our first hike will be a loop around, Lake Chabot, a popular recreation spot in the East Bay Area. It is about 30 miles from Menlo Park or a 30-40 minute drive. There are dozens of miles of scenic trails around the lake, perfect for hiking, running, and biking. The difficulty of the trails varies from paved and smooth to hilly making the terrain easy to moderate without extremes. Alltrails Map: https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/lake-chabot-west-shore-bass-cove-columbine-honker-bay-east-shore-loop  This exact trail is only 8.7 miles so your hiking plan will need to include something extra to get us to 10 miles.  There are lots of trails around the lake we could use for a small detour to add some distance.Parking: This is a popular recreation area and parking in the main lot fills up quickly.  There is plenty of parking on Lake Chabot Road and it is just a short walk to the park.Start and End Times: We will meet at Marina Café on West Shore Trail at 8:15AM.   The goal of starting the hike no later than 8:30AM.  Estimated hiking time is 5 hours including breaks and lunch which means we should wrap up and be ready to drive home between 1:30 – 2:00.  Which means leaving Menlo around 7:40AM and returning around 2:30PM...if all goes as planned!What to bring: Everyone hiking should have a day pack with 10 essentials including at least 3 liters of water, trail snacks and a sack lunch.  Wear proper shoes!!!Transportation:  Carpooling is encouraged.  Feel free to arrange a carpool on your own or you can use this google sheet.  If a parent is going on the hike and is WILLING TO DRIVE other scouts or parents, please sign up.  If scouts or parents NEED/WANT A RIDE, sign up in the same google sheet.Cheers, Ms. Atkins

events.summary: Hiking Merit Badge 10-Miler SF Urban Hike
events.description: Hiking Merit Badge 10-miler #2:  Urban Hike in SF Mark your calendars! Below are the details for our 2nd 10-mile hike we will do as a group for the Hiking Merit Badge. A few quick reminders:**Send me your written hiking plan BEFORE the hike. The template is attached. hikingtripplan.pdf**If you are not able to attend THIS hike on THIS day, that is OK. You can use the same info below to plan the hike on another day that works for your family and hopefully other scout friends.**If you want to do a different 10-mile hike, contact me to discuss your plan.**If you do this trail or another one on your own, please take 4 pictures of yourself and the group you are hiking with at different points along the trail. Include the ocean/beach, park, or other landmark in the background.**If you have not already signed up for hiking merit badge and you would like to, please email Mrs.Todd (kathrynt@gmail.com) and copy me (atkins.kerri@icloud.com).** ** ** ** ** **Our 2nd 10-mile hike will be on a trail that is a loop connecting several beautiful parks in northern San Francisco including Golden Gate Park, Land’s End, the Presidio, and Mountain Lake Park. The trail includes diverse terrain from pavement to dirt and sand.  Be prepared for windy conditions and wear layers. Note that this trail is a little over 11 miles so we will make a small adjustment to be closer to 10 miles. In your hiking plan, you can propose an adjustment to the route that will bring it closer to 10 miles.  All Trails Map:  https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/golden-gate-park-lands-end-and-presidio-loop?p=-1&sh=mzvbym&utm_medium=trail_share&utm_source=alltrails_viralityStart and End Times: We will meet at 8:45AM at the beach parking lot ACROSS from Beach Chalet with the goal of starting the hike no later than 9:00AM.  Estimated hiking time is ~5 hours including breaks and lunch which means we should wrap up and be ready to drive home between 2:00 – 2:30. Transportation:  Please use this this google sheet to indicate to indicate parents that are going on the hike and are WILLING TO DRIVE other scouts or parents.  Use this same sheet to indicate scouts or parents that NEED/WANT A RIDE.  Once we know which parents are going on the hike and the number of seats available, then we will come up with a plan, so everyone has a ride. Parking: We will start and end our hike at the corner of Fulton and Great Highway.  There is plenty of parking long the beach across from Beach Chalet.   The address for Beach Chalet is 1000 Great Highway but do not park in the lot that is near the restaurant…park across the street in the beach lot.What to bring: Everyone hiking should have a day pack with 10 essentials including at least 3 liters of water, trail snacks and a sack lunch.  Wear proper shoes!!!

Suggested Packing ListWe will MEET at Menlo Church no later than 4:30 pm, to gather necessary gear from the Troop Shed, and depart in carpool shortly after. We plan to return early in the evening on Saturday August 23 (~5:00 pm). We will let you know if we plan to depart any earlier (on Saturday).  - Do not forget to bring a swimsuit (+ towel and sun protection). It is projected to be 91°F this weekend (hot), and we will hopefully be swimming by the time it gets that hot!This retreat is intended for the Patrol Leaders' Coucil (PLC) and upcoming youth leaders. It’s all about building a strong, connected team, learning how to lead effectively, and bringing those NYLT lessons to life in our troop.But it's not all serious business - we're also bringing the fun: Campfire cooking with recipes inspired by the Outdoor Boys Swimming Time to explore and relax in the beautiful Santa Cruz MountainsReferences: Scout Leadership Retreat Google Drive Folder

events.summary: California Coastal Day - Conservation Volunteer Opportunity
events.description: TO SIGN UP: 1 - RSVP TO THIS TROOPTRACK EVENT (so we know who to expect) 2 - SIGN UP THROUGH EVENTBRITE AT THIS WEBPAGE: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/2025-coastal-cleanup-day-at-san-francisquito-creek-registration-1493192592239?aff=SMcounty and 3 - Complete the digital waiver form: English Waiver | Español Waiver. You will receive a copy of your waiver via email once complete. Save this email to show to your site captain. *Or* print and sign a waiver and bring it with you to the cleanup: https://www.smchealth.org/sites/main/files/file-attachments/2025_waiver_english_short_form_custom_waiver_0.pdf?1753281625~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~Join us as we help in a conservation project which is happening across California. This year we will be "taking trash out of San Francisquito Creek."This is a great opportunity to earn conservation service hours very locally. We will be working with Grassroots Ecology and the City of Menlo Park.Here is critical information:The City of Menlo Park and Grassroots Ecology are partnering to host over 100 volunteers for Coastal Cleanup Day at San Francisquito Creek. This annual event brings communities together throughout the state to clean California's beaches, lakes, and watersheds. Join us as we remove trash and recyclables from our local watershed to celebrate and enhance our beautiful waterways.Waivers/Registration: All volunteers attending must SIGN TWO WAIVERS. SEE ABOVE FOR THE LINKS. Youth under 18 must have their legal guardian approve the waivers on their behalf. Volunteers age 12 and under require an accompanying adult.Parking: Street parking available along Alma St. next to the train tracks. There is no nearby official bike parking, but you can leave it at the meeting location – there will be at least one staff to look over it.Prepare for Picking up Trash Outdoors:Face mask for COVID-19 safety (optional)Reusable water bottle (filled with water)Snacks, if desiredLong pants, long sleeves recommendedSturdy closed-toed shoes that can get dirtyHat/sunscreen or rain gear/bootsEpi-Pen or allergy medicine, if applicablePlan for potential exposure to sun, poison oak, ticks, prickly plantsOptional (but highly encouraged) Things to Bring:BucketGardening glovesTrash picker, or large long tongsShovel (for digging buried shopping carts out of the creek bed)The organizers will provide light snacks, water jug, disposable gloves, trash bags, and a limited number of trash pickers.

events.summary: Hiking Merit Badge - Windy Hill 10-mile Hike
events.description: WindyHill3Trails-HikingMB.pdfHiking Merit Badge 10-miler #3:  Windy Hill to Alpine Inn (Modified Route++)Mark your calendars! Below are the details for our 3rd 10-mile hike for the Hiking Merit Badge. A few quick reminders:**Send me your written hiking plan BEFORE the hike. The template is attached. hikingtripplan.pdf**If you are not able to attend THIS hike on THIS day, that is OK. You can use the same info below to plan the hike on another day that works for your family and hopefully other scout friends.**If you want to do another/different 10-mile hike contact me to discuss your plan.**If you this Windy Hill hike on a different day, please take 4 pictures of yourself and the group you are hiking with at different points along the trail. Include the trailhead, view of the bay, or other landmark in the background.**If you have not already signed up for hiking merit badge and you would like to, please email Mrs. Todd (kathrynt@gmail.com) and copy me (atkins.kerri@icloud.com).Cheers, Ms. Atkins** ** ** ** ** **For our 3rd 10 mile hike, we will be piecing together 3 trails to give us 10 miles of some challenging, sunny, uphill trails and some scenic, shaded, downhill trails.Alltrails Maps: Trail #1: Spring Ridge Trail to Windy Hill (~3.0) https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/spring-ridge-trail-to-windy-hillTrail #2:  Windy Hill to Hamms Gulch (~4.5 miles)  https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/hamms-gulch-trail--2Trail #3: Toyon to Old Spanish Trail to Alpine Inn   https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/toyon-trail-old-spanish-trail-and-coal-mine-ridge-loopWhat to bring: Please wear proper hiking shoes!!!  Not only is this a hike with elevation and switchback trails, the sneakers you wear to school are not appropriate for hiking AND they are likely to be ruined.  We are getting into the rainy season so the chance of rain and therefore mud is higher.  Everyone hiking should have a day pack with 10 essentials including at least 2 liters of water, trail snacks.  Sack lunch in optional given the option to eat lunch at Alpine Inn. And don't forget a copy of the attached map. Transportation:  The trailhead is ~12 miles from Menlo Park depending on where you live. Please use this google sheet to indicate if you (scout or parent) need a ride. This same google sheet should be used to indicate parents that are going on the hike and are WILLING TO DRIVE other scouts or parents. Parking: We will park at the Spring Ridge Trailhead located here https://maps.app.goo.gl/tkmKBSurBokh6TXP8 .  There is limited parking so consider carpooling.  We will make a decision whether to finish at the trailhead parking lot or Alpine Inn based on interest grabbing a bite to eat after the hike. Start and End Times: Since this hike is so close to home we will start a little earlier.  We will meet at the parking lot at the Spring Ridge trail head Lot at 8:30AM with the goal of starting the hike no later than 8:45AM.  Estimated hiking time is ~4-4.5 hours which means we should wrap up around 1:00PM.

events.summary: Hiking MB:  10-Mile Group Hike @ Halfmoon Bay / California Coastal Trail++
events.description: Hiking Merit Badge 10-miler #4:  California Coastal Trail++Mark your calendars! Below are the details for our 4th 10-mile hike for the Hiking Merit Badge. A few quick reminders:**Send me your written hiking plan BEFORE the hike. The template is attached. hikingtripplan.pdf**If you are not able to attend THIS hike on THIS day, that is OK. You can use the same info below to plan the hike on another day that works for your family and hopefully other scout friends.**If you want to do another/different 10-mile hike contact me to discuss your plan.**If you do the California Coastal Trail hike on a different day, please take 4 pictures of yourself and the group you are hiking with at different points along the trail. Include the ocean/beach, hotel, or other landmark in the background.**If you have not already signed up for hiking merit badge and you would like to, please email Mrs. Todd (kathrynt@gmail.com) and copy me (atkins.kerri@icloud.com).** ** ** ** ** **For our 4th 10-mile hike (WOW!!), we will be piecing together 3 trails to give us 10 miles of amazing views of the Pacific Ocean:  Cowell Purisima, Halfmoon Bay Coastal Trail, and California Coastal Trail.  Each of these trails are featured individually as Out and Back hikes—BUT if we do them each as a point to point, then they link together to make one long 10-mile hike.Alltrails Maps: Cowell Purisima:  Point to Point 3.5 miles   https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/cowell-purisima-trail--2Halfmoon Bay Coastal Trail: Point to Point 1.4 miles   https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/half-moon-bay-coastal-trail--2California Coastal Trail: Point to Point 5.2 miles   https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/california-coastal-trail--2Transportation:  Carpooling is essential for this hike because our starting point is 10 miles from the end point.   Ideally, we would have a combination of parents that are hiking and parents that are NOT hiking who could drop us off and/or help us shuttle cars from the starting point to the end point.  Please use this this google sheet to indicate if you are willing to help us with transportation.  I will circle back with the transportation plan, once we firm up which scouts and parents are hiking and if any additional parents can help with just driving/carpool. Parking: There is limited parking at the starting point which is the trailhead for Cowell-Purisima.  We will finish ~10 miles away in the beach town of Miramar.  We will meet in and around the corner of Magellan Ave and Mirada Road where there is a fair bit of parking but it is in various spots along the side streets. Start and End Times: We will meet at the Cowell-Purisima Parking Lot at 8:45AM.   The goal of starting the hike no later than 9:00AM.  Estimated hiking time is ~5 hours including breaks and lunch which means we should wrap up and be ready to drive home between 2:00 – 2:30. What to bring: Everyone hiking should have a day pack with 10 essentials including at least 3 liters of water, trail snacks and a sack lunch.  Wear proper shoes!!!  And we will hike rain or shine.  Check weather before the hike but plan to bring a rain jacket, an extra warm layer, and a sunhat.

events.description: *** This outing has been cancelled due to illness of adult leaders, and not enough time to find an alternative ****Coyote Lake Car Camping is coming up! One more week to sign up for this 2-night car camping outing. If you don't know, car camping just means we don't need to hike in. We can drive up to the campsite. Outing Senior Patrol Leader (SPL): Tomas Jaramillo-Lopez Primary Adult Coordinator: Jon AvrachOnce again we set off for nearby Coyote Lake Campground. There is mountain biking there, so add a note in your RSVP if you'd like to do that, so we can arrange for bike transport. There are also some beautiful hiking trails and we'll likely be able to have a campfire.If you need the 10 mile hike for the Hiking Merit Badge, this is a great place to get that done. Plan the route in advance and submit it to your Merit Badge Counselor. Then let the SPL (Tomas) know we want to take that hike on Saturday.In total, 13 spots for scouts and 3 spots for adults (1 of which is an ASM) are available.PACKING LIST There will be no official pack check. If this is your first time camping with the Troop or you need a Pack Check for advancement, reach out to the SPL.Coyote Lake WebsiteCoyote Lake 2025 Outing Cooking Sign Up Form - If you are a SCOUT and need cooking requirements signed off, fill this out to indicate you need to cook on an outing. Outing Leadership will email you and confirm if you are cooking 2 weeks before the outing.Coyote Lake 2025 Carpool & Tenting Planning Form - If you are an ADULT and planning to attend outing, please fill this out to help with coordinating this effort. Primary Adult Coordinator will reach out to you prior to outing with transportation assignments and route plan. RSVP DEADLINE & Waitlist: The current RSVP Deadline is set for Friday Oct 31 to allow Scouts in leadership to adequately prepare for the outing, which requires having accurate headcount of participants to plan around. If the maximum capacity is reached, please fill out the following form to be placed on the waitlist if a spot opens up:Coyote Lake 2025 Waitlist

[MEETING RECORDING LINK]High Sierra 50-Miler PresentationHello Troop 222 Families,It’s hard to believe how quickly 2025 is wrapping up - and just as exciting is what’s on the horizon: Troop 222’s first 50-Miler Backpacking Trek since before COVID-19! This long-standing Troop tradition (and one of my personal favorites) will take place July 26 - August 2, 2026, in the beautiful High Sierras of the Inyo National Forest.This is an invitation to join an Informational Meeting regarding this expedition. There are a lot of moving parts and logistics that make this different than most of our normal outings. We will aim for 30 minutes informational presentation, and a portion for an open format Q&A. We will also record this meeting and post the informational resources. Important:For Scouts already confirmed on the crew, this meeting is MANDATORY. (Parents of Scouts are also REQUIRED to attend).The meeting is also open to all Scouts who are interested or considering joining the waitlist to learn more about training expectations, gear requirements, and the trek itself. (Parents you are also HIGHLY encouraged to attend).If you’d like to be considered for an open spot, please add your name to the [SCOUT WAITLIST LINK]. Should space become available - or if we are able to form a second crew - spots will be offered on a first-come, first-served basis.Troop 222 remains committed to ensuring that any Scout who demonstrates the required preparation, skills, and commitment has the opportunity to participate. This trek is challenging, rewarding, and truly transformational, and we want every interested Scout to feel informed and ready.

events.summary: Hiking Merit Badge 20 Mile Hike:  SF Bay Trail
events.description: Hello Hiking MB families!!!Mark your calendars for Sunday, December 14! Below are the details for the FINAL group hike for Hiking Merit Badge 2025: 20 miles in 1 day!! **Send me your written hiking plan BEFORE the hike. The template is attached. hikingtripplan.pdf**If you are not able to attend THIS hike on THIS day, that is OK. You can use the same info below to plan the hike on another day that works for your family and hopefully other scout friends.**If you want to do another/different 20-mile hike contact me to discuss your plan.**If you do a 20-mile hike on a different day, please take 4 pictures of yourself and the group you are hiking with at different points along the trail. Include the starting point, ocean/bay/lake views, bridges, parks or other landmarks.**Remember you MUST finish all 4 of your 10-mile hikes BEFORE December 14 if you want to join us on this 20-mile hike.  ** ** ** ** ** **This hike will take us along a small stretch of the Bay Trail, which is currently more than 350 miles of trails connecting Bay Area communities, parks, open spaces, schools and transit. When it’s complete, this trail will include a total of 500 miles for hiking, walking, running or biking through 47 cities, across 7 toll bridges, along shorelines, BBQ spots, and over 130 parks.For your hiking plan, you will be piecing together 3 AllTrails routes. We will start just south of SFO airport, wind along the SF Bay, weave through Foster City and Redwood Shores, before following sidewalks across the 101 for the final stretch into San Carlos. The Alltrails routes below total 24.8 miles so your hiking plan should make a recommendation on where to shorten the route to get us closer to 20 miles.Alltrails Maps: San Francisco Bay Trail - 10.7 miles Point to Point:   https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/san-francisco-bay-trail-foster-city-and-san-mateo-bikewaysFoster City Levee Trail -  4.8 miles Point to Point: https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/foster-city-levee-trail-belmont-sloughRedwood Shores Loop Trail - 7.8 mile Loop:  https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/california/redwood-shores-loop-trailFinal Stretch to San Carlos – 1.5 miles Point to Point: There is no official trail to refer to but using google maps you can determine how to use sidewalks and the bridge over the 101 to make our way to downtown San Carlos Train Station  (for a celebration picture!!).Transportation:  We need parents that are NOT hiking to sign up to drop off and/or pick up adults and scouts who are hiking.  Please use this this google sheet to indicate if you are willing to help us with transportation.  I will circle back with the transportation plan, once we firm up the parents that are willing to help with just driving/carpool. Drop off will be in the parking lot at The Westin SFO Airport, 1 Old Bayshore Hwy, Millbrae, CA 94030Pick up will be in downtown San Carlos @ Blue Line Pizza 1201 San Carlos Ave, San Carlos, CA 94070Start and End Times: This hike will take ~ 9 -10 hours including breaks, lunch, etc.  The goal is to meet at starting point at 8:00 with the goal of starting the hike no later than 8:15AM which should have us in San Carlos at 5:30ish. What to bring: Everyone hiking should have a day pack with at least 3 liters of water and trail snacks.  Wear proper shoes!!!  And we will hike rain or shine. Check weather before the hike but plan to bring a rain jacket, an extra warm layer, a sunhat, and sunglasses.

events.summary: ⛷️🏂 Ski and Snowboard Trip to Northstar - January 17-19
events.description: NORTHSTAR SKIING 2026 PHOTO ALBUMRegistration is now open to everyone, scouts and adults. Max of 24 people total.Carpool Signup Form (fill this out if you are an adult)Cooking Signup FormPacking ListThe Ski and Snowboarding trip is backWe have two big VRBO rentals in Northstar with a shuttle to take you to the Village and the slopes. There is a sledding area for those who prefer not to ski or snowboard.Lift tickets and equipment rentalBring your own lift ticket and ski or snowboard equipment. Lift tickets are available now. They are far less expensive when purchased well in advance online at the Northstar Lift Tickets site. Buy a 2-day pass that includes Peak Dates, which allows you to ski for two days in any three-day period. I wasn't able to find a reliable source for discount tickets. If you know a season pass holder, they may be able to provide you a Buddy pass that enables you to get a discount.You will need to rent equipment on your own if you don't own it. There are cost-effective rentals at Sports Basement. This will also allow you to help your scout find the appropriate equipment. There will be no adult from the Troop to help them in the onsite ski rental.Bring a sleeping bag and pad to sleep on. The house we are renting has many rooms and beds, but depending on the configuration, it's possible we'll need to adjust based on ages and genders. This might create an indoor camping opportunity for some of us! And you'll be warmer with a sleeping bag, even if you're in a bed.We are meeting at 6:00am sharp on Saturday, January 16 at Menlo Church, so we can get some skiing in that day. Bring a bag lunch and some money to stop for lunch on the way home (and for snacks while skiing).There is room for 24 guests only. 6 Adults and 18 scouts. Adult spots first need to be filled by 1 trained ASM and 1 female adult. After that other parents who are registered with the Troop may sign up if there is space. *** We are holding 6 total spots for adults. ***As adults sign up, I will release any spots that are left for scouts to register. (This is to avoid having scouts take all the spots and then needing to turn them away to make space for the adults we need to have.)Reminders:Please bring a copy of your A  and B med forms if we do not already have them on file from a prior trip this scouting year.If you need to cancel, there will be no refunds, so think carefully before scheduling!

events.summary: Connor Decatur Eagle Project - Discovery Shop Beautification Project
events.location: 1032 Henderson Avenue, Menlo Park, CA 94025, The Discovery Shop, 748 Santa Cruz Ave, Menlo Park, CA 94025
events.description: On January 17th and 18th, I will be organizing my Eagle Scout service project in partnership with the Discovery Shop in downtown Menlo Park. The project will take place over two days and will involve the construction and installation of three DIY planters, as well as beautifying the Discovery Shop’s back entrance by removing and reorganizing weeds and painting a metal box white.                                          The Discovery Shop is a nonprofit resale store operated by the American Cancer Society, with all proceeds supporting its mission to fight cancer. Funds raised help support cancer research, patient services, education, and advocacy efforts nationwide. The American Cancer Society’s cause is to save lives, celebrate lives, and lead the fight for a world without cancer.                                          This project will benefit the Discovery Shop by improving accessibility to donated items, enhancing the appearance of the back entrance to encourage donations, and creating better use of the space. Removing soil and weeds will also help eliminate potential tripping hazards caused by uneven bricks and debris, making the area safer for volunteers and donors.                                          Below, you can sign up for the day(s) you would like to participate. The more help, the better! Please note that adults will be present at all times to oversee safety protocols and ensure proper tool use. I do need Registered Leaders to help, as well.                                          SIGN UP HERE                                          Day 1 – Planter Construction                                          When: January 17th, 2025 | 12:30 PM – 3:30 PMWhere: Mr. Fichou’s House (1032 Henderson Ave, Menlo Park CA 94025)                                          Day 2 – Planter Installation & Beautification                                          When: January 18th, 2025 | 10:00 AM – 12:00 PMWhere: Discovery Shop, Menlo Park (748 Santa Cruz Ave, Menlo Park, CA 94025)                                          Sincerely,                           Connor Decatur                                           Troop 222

events.description: Clarification: if you are NOT on a 50-Miler Crew, DO NOT RSVP for this event. RSVP for the general troop outing [HERE]Hello 50-Miler Participants,The 50-Miler is officially 190 days away, and our first required prep hike is less than one month out. As discussed in the Informational Meeting, attendance at the Prep Outings is REQUIRED:There are three (3) total prep outingsYou may miss one (1) outing only if absolutely necessaryMissing two (2) outings will result in removal from the 50-MilerPlease RSVP to this invitation as soon as possible.-----------------------------------------------------------Mandatory Pack Check [Please RSVP for the Pack Check (CLICK HERE)]In addition to the prep hike, there will be a MANDATORY Pack Check:📅 January 29⏰ 6:00–7:00 PM (before the Troop Meeting)📍 Troop ShedThese pack checks are essential for safety and performance. Reducing base pack weight is a top priority, and each pack check will be thorough and detailed to eliminate unnecessary items.If you are unable to attend, you must notify us ASAP at 50-miler2026@t222.org📎 Please review the Packing List linked here.-----------------------------------------------------------Virtual Pack Check (Required Before In-Person Check DUE 1/25)Before the in-person Pack Check, all participants must complete a Virtual Pack Check. A Virtual Pack Check consists of:A photo of all gear that would go in your packItems laid out clearly, similar to the attached reference photo📅 Submission deadline: End of day Sunday, January 25📎 Reference Photo Attached: Virtual Pack Check Reference PhotoPrep Hike #1 Details📅 February 14–16, 2026🕒 3 Days / 2 Nights (~16 miles total)📍 Route: Bear Valley → Wildcat Camp → Coast Camp → Bear Valley⚖️ Difficulty: ModerateHighlights include:Forest canyon hiking, Coastal bluffs and ocean views, Beach access, Optional waterfall hikeWe look forward to kicking off our training and preparing for a successful and safe 50-Miler. If you have any questions regarding 50-Miler preparation, please contact the Adult Leadership Team at 50-miler2026@t222.org.

Join the Troop for our first backpacking outing of 2026 to one of our absolute favorite local locations! Point Reyes National Seashore!Clarification: if you are on a 50-Miler Crew, DO NOT RSVP for this event. [RSVP HERE]This outing features coastal bluff hiking, optional trips to Alamere Falls, time for Scout skill modules, and a calm basecamp experience. Our trip is now set for one night at Wildcat Camp, Scouts carry full packs into and from the camp.https://www.nps.gov/thingstodo/hike-to-wildcat-beach.htmhttps://www.nps.gov/pore/planyourvisit/wildcat-campground.htmOur Hiking Plan:Bear Valley Visitor Center → Wildcat CampMileage: ~6 milesRoute: Bear Valley Trail → Glen Trail → Ocean Lake Loop → Wildcat CampTrail & Camp ExpectationsGentle uphill through shaded redwood and fern-filled forestOpens gradually into coastal bluffs with wide ocean viewsWildcat Camp sits directly above Wildcat BeachWater spigot, picnic tables, and food lockers availableThe rest of the itinerary is in the process of being developed by Outing Leadership, can read about ideas [HERE]If you are a SCOUT and want to cook and/or need cooking requirements signed off, fill out this [Point Reyes Outing Cooking Sign Up Form] to indicate you need to cook on an outing. Outing Leadership will email you and confirm if you are cooking 2 weeks before the outing.If you are an ADULT and planning to attend outing, please fill out [Point Reyes Carpool & Tenting Planning Form] to help with coordinating this effort. Primary Adult Coordinator will reach out to you prior to outing with transportation assignments and route plan. RSVP DEADLINE & Waitlist: The current RSVP Deadline is set for Friday Jan 30 to allow Scouts in leadership to adequately prepare for the outing, which requires having accurate headcount of participants to plan around. If the maximum capacity is reached, please fill out the following form to be placed on the waitlist if a spot opens up:[Point Reyes Waitlist]Full Disclosure: This time of year, Point Reyes is subject to cooler temps, rain, and wind - nothing our prepared Scouts and Adult Leaders can't handle though!Leadership will be keeping an eye on the weather [NOAA Weather Forecast] and communicating with NPS Personnel to clarify conditions if necessary.
Reminder that we will not have a meeting, as many schools are closed this week.

events.description: The PLC is the troop’s elected and duly appointed governing body. Resting on their shoulders is the planning, preparation, and presentation of the troop’s Scouting program. It’s up to them to see that the promise of Scouting is delivered. Though this might appear to be a daunting proposition, with the proper training, they can gain the required tools and skills needed to run things in a fashion that results in a thriving program benefitting all the members of their troop.

Diego (Nurse) and Stockton (Paramedic, Wilderness EMT) are offering a Wilderness First Aid and CPR  course for T222 adults and scouts (1st class and up). Participants can receive certification in Wilderness First Aid and Adult CPR (you must be 14 years old to receive certification). Additionally, scouts who complete the course will earn the First Aid Merit Badge. Participants must attend both weekend sessions to receive the Wilderness First Aid certification. Seats are limited.Times:Thu, Feb 5 | 7:00–8:30 PM (CPR portion, regular scout meeting)Sat, Feb 28 | 9:00 AM–5:30 PMSun, Mar 1 | 9:00 AM–5:30 PMCost: $50 – covers course materials and a personal first-aid kit for you to keep$30 for certification (this is paid to HSI, the certifying agency, and will be collected later)

This is a formal recognition with families, friends, and the public in attendance. All Scouts who have moved up to any rank except Eagle Scout, or who have earned merit badges since the last court of honor, should be recognized.

The Troop Committee is a cross between a board of directors and a parents support group. It sets troop policies and handles administrative functions, allowing the Scoutmaster and Assistant Scoutmasters (ASMs) to focus on working directly with the Scouts. A robust troop committee supports an effective scouting program.AGENDA & MEETING MINUTESAll adults are welcome to attend committee meetings - regardless of whether they hold an official leadership role. We value diverse perspectives and appreciate constructive feedback that helps strengthen our troop. If you have ideas or suggestions to improve troop operations, please share them using our TROOP COMMITTEE SUGGESTION TRACKER.The committee meets monthly on the second Tuesday of each month to stay informed and provide consistent support to the program. events.summary: Adult Meeting - “Our Troop, Our Journey: Looking Ahead Together”
events.description: Hello Troop 222 Families,As we move past the halfway point of the program year, it’s a good time to pause, reflect, and look ahead.Our Scouts have logged real miles, taken on new responsibilities, and continued progressing in rank and skills. That kind of growth doesn’t happen by accident, it happens when families and leaders are aligned in purpose and expectations.We’re inviting all Registered Adults and any parents interested in deeper involvement, to attend a focused IN PERSON Adult Leadership Meeting.What we’ll cover:A brief “State of the Troop” updateHow the Scouting program is designed to develop character and leadershipExpectations for Registered Adult participationPreparation standards for high-adventure opportunities (e.g. 50-Miler 2026 & Sea Base 2027)Clarification of adult dues reimbursement requirementsOpportunities to step into roles such as Assistant Scoutmaster & Committee This won’t be a long administrative meeting. The goal is clarity, alignment, and momentum, especially as we continue building a strong, youth-led troop.If you’ve ever wondered:How can I better support my Scout?What does the troop need most from adults right now?Should I consider stepping into a more active leadership role?This meeting will answer those questions.We know schedules are full, and we’re grateful for the time you already invest. Your presence helps ensure we continue building a troop that challenges our Scouts, develops real leadership, and prepares them for meaningful high adventure.[PRESENTATION SLIDES]Troop 222 is inviting you to a scheduled Zoom meeting.Topic: Our Troop, Our Journey: Looking Ahead TogetherTime: Mar 12, 2026 07:00 PM Pacific Time (US and Canada)Join Zoom Meetinghttps://us02web.zoom.us/j/88151673350?pwd=f88Ow4mEqnt1a6UlCBNvItQHFozjAs.1Meeting ID: 881 5167 3350Passcode: 051214Thank you for being part of it.Diego Sabio Paese Committee Chair - t222.org dsabiopaese@t222.org (650)-815-8805

We're going Backpacking! 🎒🎒🎒🥾🥾🥾We have reserved the beautiful Star Mines group camp at Black Diamond Mines. The park is in Somersville, CA near Mount Diablo. This is the same campsite we've had in the past but they are now allowing backpacking to the site again! 🎉It is a 3 1/2 mile hike each way with some hills.Cooking SignupCarpool Form (all adults need to fill this)Packing ListFor more information on Black Diamond Mines Preserve, see their website.Meet at 7:00AM at the Menlo Church near the shed. Make sure to bring rain gear, and 2-3 liters of water.We will do a minimal pack check specifically for rain gear and personal water supply before we leave.We will camp overnight for one nights and leave after breakfast on Sunday. This is a dry camp, so we will be able to drive some water in by car, but please bring 2-3 liters of water for yourself for hiking.Great opportunity for Cooking requirements because we can use backpacking stoves to get the trail cooking requirements.No fires - Camp stoves only.Minimum of 17 people and a Maximum of 35 people for this group campsite. One adult per 3-5 scouts will provide enough drivers. Adults, if you sign up, plan to drive scouts and gear.

🏔️ 50-Miler Prep: Mandatory Pack Check #2📅 Date: THURSDAY March 19⏰ Time: 6:00–7:00 PM (Sharp! Before the Troop Meeting)📍 Location: Troop ShedGoing lightweight isn't just for gear junkies - it’s how you survive a 50-mile trek with a smile on your face. These pack checks are essential for your safety and performance. We will be doing a thorough, item-by-item review to help you eliminate "dead weight" and dial in your kit. BRING YOUR PACK, HIKING SHOES, TREKKING POLES, EVERYTHING, as if you are going on the outing that night! 📋 YOUR MISSION: Own Your GearThis year, we are empowering YOU to take ownership of your trek prep. Every Scout is responsible for weighing their own gear and logging it into our new system.Find Your Crew: We have TWO crews this summer. Check the table below to see which crew you are on.Open Your Spreadsheet: Select the link for your specific Crew Spreadsheet (in the table)Log Your Gear: Use a kitchen scale to weigh your items in ounces. Follow the attached [GEAR LIST INSTRUCTIONS] for a step-by-step guide.The 15lb Challenge: Can you get your Base Weight (everything except food, water, and fuel) under 15 lbs? [PACKING LIST]Re: TREKKING POLES, they deserve to be treated as standard safety equipment for ALL backpackers - they reduce joint strain and the risk of musculoskeletal injury, which is why most experienced backpackers use them. We will also be using them as troop gear (for the tents we are taking on the trek), and need to ensure we have enough for the crew on this outing. Pro-Tip: Check out [Stockton’s Gear Recommendations] for ideas on how to cut weight and quality gear recommendations.🏆 THE 50-MILER AWARDSWe aren't just weighing gear for safety we’re doing it for glory! We will be tracking your progress in the spreadsheets and handing out BIG PRIZES at our FINAL PACK CHECK before we leave for the High Sierra.Awards will be given for:🥇 The Ounce Counter: Most detailed and 100% complete spreadsheet.🥈 Lean & Mean: Best weight-to-bodyweight ratio (most efficient pack for your size).🥉 The MacGyver: Most creative or clever "ultralight" DIY gear item.click here for [Agassiz GEAR LIST]click here for [Emigrant GEAR LIST]Jacob AtkinsEthan ArguelloChase BrownHenry AtkinsAdrian FeodorovKinsley BrownAditi NarayananFin CurtisRomy Chase RoyaltyLeo GallagherNeil Yeruva Miller HartsteinRiva YeruvaSilvi KarpatiStockton RayTrinton LeDiego Sabio PaeseRiley YuMirela SavinKerri AtkinsRaj YeruvaEmmet AvrachBrent BrownPhil HartsteinNguyen Le⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTESPreparation: Please try to log as much gear as possible in your spreadsheet before MARCH 19. We will be verifying your Base Weight at the shed.Attendance: If you cannot attend, you must notify the leadership team ASAP at 50-miler2026@t222.org.We look forward to continue our training! If you have questions, reach out to the Adult Leadership Team at 50-miler2026@t222.org.

events.summary: Dutch Oven Cooking - Hands on training with eating! 👨🏻‍🍳🔥🥘
events.description: Join us in Flood Park to learn Dutch Oven cooking hands on. You will be making Moussaka and Peach Cobbler from scratch, cooking in the Dutch Oven and using a chimney and coals. We will meet in the lovely Maple Group Area, which has standup grills, like we enjoyed on the Memorial Park outing, and plenty of seating. It's recommended to bring your mess kit, to get an authentic camping meal experience. Best of all, we'll finish up with eating your lunch creations as a group. If you are vegetarian or have food allergies, note that in your comments when you respond, so we can plan accordingly.This day activity is limited to up to 30 people who RSVP so we can plan for the right amount of food, so grab your spot today!Note: This is the ideal preparation for cooking one of the meals in your Cooking Merit Badge requirement 5d, which is:In the outdoors, using your menu plans and recipes for this requirement, cook two of the four meals you planned using either a camp stove OR backpacking stove. Use a skillet OR a Dutch oven over campfire coals for the third meal, and cook the fourth meal in a foil pack OR on a skewer. Serve all of these meals to your patrol or a group of youth.

This meeting is OPEN TO ALL SCOUTS AND ADULTS who wish to attend and provide input on a current proposed purchase of Troop funds. The objective of this meeting is to reach a consensus on a unified gear and cooking strategy that balances the Patrol Method with the 25% Body Weight Safety Goal for the 50-Miler Trek.This is a non-biased forum designed to ensure everyone feels heard before the Committee makes a final decision. We will be following a structured agenda to respect everyone’s time, followed by a formal vote from the Committee and trained ASMs to finalize our path forward.Troop 222 Gear & Program Forum AGENDAThis meeting will be held virtually: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/88428963329?pwd=ZDEqaf8Fzae7mAqS3IEzVjbjLUFduc.1

We consider other decisions that the PLC should be involved in.The PLC is the troop’s elected and duly appointed governing body. Resting on their shoulders is the planning, preparation, and presentation of the troop’s Scouting program. It’s up to them to see that the promise of Scouting is delivered. Though this might appear to be a daunting proposition, with the proper training, they can gain the required tools and skills needed to run things in a fashion that results in a thriving program benefitting all the members of their troop.

TL\;DR: 50-Miler Skills Day The Goal: Master the "Arrival Workflow." Scouts will rotate through stations to ensure they can set up camp efficiently, safely, and independently before we hit the trail.The Stations & Link to ItineraryStation 1 (Shelter): Durston X-Mid 2 mastery. Perfecting the "Rectangle Pitch" on tough terrain.Station 2 (The Rigging): Mastery of the Big 3 knots: Bowline, Taut-line, and Trucker’s Hitch.Station 3 (Water): Filter field-testing and maintaining the "Dirty vs. Clean" water system.Station 4 (Kitchen/Bears): Independent stove operation and "Smellable" management in bear canisters.Bonus (Group Ops): Setting up the REI Half Dome group tarp with technical rigging.The Workflow: This isn't just practice—it’s a rehearsal of exactly what we do the moment we arrive at a campsite after a long day of hiking.Where to meet: Flood Park Parking Lot by the Oak Premium Picnic Site, we will walk around and find a site for us to work with. Please be on time. Call Diego (6508158805) if you are having trouble finding it. What to Bring: Trekking PolesMess Kit (you will eat the food you cook)Personal Water Bottle Sun protection

50-Miler Prep Outing #2 – Ohlone Wilderness Trail (Fremont → Livermore)Our next 50-Miler Prep Outing is coming up soon, and this will be our most challenging preparation hike yet. How you prepare over the next few weeks will make a big difference.Prepare NowThink about the Point Reyes outing (or your last backpacking trip):What gear worked well?What didn’t work?What can you remove to lower your pack weight?Crew Challenge: Can you get your base weight under 15 lbs? (pack before troop gear, food, water, etc.) Click HERE for [Stockton's Gear Recommendations]Start training now by hiking with your backpack.Windy Hill is a great local training hike.Attendance Requirements (RSVP Yes OR No)To participate in the 50-Miler, Scouts must attend 2 of the 3 prep outings.If you did NOT attend Point Reyes, you must attend:This outingThe final prep outing in May (5/8-5/10)If this is an issue, email 50-miler2026@t222.org ASAP.Required Events Before the TripPack Check - March 19 (REQUIRED)Bring your gear and practice packing your backpack.Skills Day - March 29 (MANDATORY)We will practice using the gear and backcountry skills everyone will need on the trek.Driver Help NeededThis is a point-to-point hike, so we need drivers to:Drop us off in Fremont (Stanford Staging Area)Pick us up in Livermore (Lake Del Valle Family Campground)Without drivers, we cannot run the outing.Parents of scouts and partners of adult leaders - please sign up to help[SIGN UP HERE on Carpool Planning FormTrek Plan (LINK TO MAP) 3 days hiking, 2 nights, 28 miles point-to-pointDepartureMeet: Troop ShedTime: 6:00 AMDate: Saturday April 4Eat breakfast before you arrive.Day 1: Mission Peak → Sunol Backpack Camp~11 miles~3,000 ft elevation gainCamp at Sunol Backpack CampDay 2: Sunol → Maggie’s Half Acre (near Rose Peak)~7 miles~2,500 ft gainHigh-elevation campsite with great viewsDay 3: Maggie’s Half Acre → Del Valle~10 milesMostly downhillLunch on trailFinish around early afternoonPickup: Del Valle Regional Park (Livermore)More details will be shared as we get closer. Questions(?) Email: 50-miler2026@gmail.com

If you’ve ever thought about getting more involved, now’s the perfect time. We’re hosting an IN-PERSON Assistant Scoutmaster Position-Specific Training with our Training Coordinator, David Fichou. This session covers the same material as the online training modules but with the added benefit of real-time discussion and Q&A.Our troop is growing (a great problem to have!), and with that growth comes the need for a few more amazing adults to join our leadership team as Assistant Scoutmasters (ASMs).ASMs play a huge role in helping our Scouts thrive - not just their own kids, but the entire troop. It truly takes a village to make this program possible, and every additional adult leader helps ensure continuity and stability as our Scouts progress through the program. New ASMs learning alongside experienced ones is how we build a smooth succession plan and keep Troop 222 strong year after year.Whether it’s mentoring youth leaders, sharing outdoor adventures, or simply being a friendly, reliable presence at meetings and outings, ASMs help everything run smoothly and safely.Click here for [Assistant Scoutmaster Roles & Responsibilities]No prior Scouting experience is needed - just a willingness to help, learn, and have fun. We’ll provide all the training and support you need!We expect you to have a lot of questions or want to clarify items. Please do not hesitate to email David Fichou (david@fichou.net) prior to the event!

events.summary: 🏖️ 🏕️ Sunset State Beach Camping April 24 4pm - April 26 1pm
events.description: We're going beach camping at Sunset State Beach!Two nights to give us time to explore the seashore. events.summary: Troop Meeting: Scavenger Hunt / Orienteering Basics
events.location: Troop Meetings,Menlo Church Fellowship Hall,Santa Cruz Ave,Menlo Park,CA
events.description: What: Scavenger Hunt / Orienteering Basics. This fun meeting will involve a combination of scavenger hunt and will cover orienteering basics in preparation for our Bayfront Bedwell Orienteering meeting on 5/14.- Preparation for First Class requirement 4a- A scavenger hunt-like activity will take place around downtown Menlo ParkBe Prepared: - Please wear warm clothes such as outerwear (eg. a jacket or sweater)- Please bring a compass if you have one- Please bring a water bottleWho: Kinsley Brown and Fin CurtisExtra Reminders: Please be on time, preferably 10 minutes minutes early so we can start and end in a timely mannerPlease refrain from entering any stores and purchasing itemsStay with your patrol at all times\; do not wander off alone

OverviewThis is our third and final overnight preparation outing before we hit the trail for our 50-Miler in July. This trip is critical: we are heading to Tahoe to simulate the high-altitude conditions we will face this summer. Your body works significantly harder at 9,000+ feet, and this is the time to learn how you react and how to pace yourself.⚠️ ATTENDANCE REQUIREMENT: To remain on the 50-Miler crew, you must have attended at least two of the three prep outings. If you have only attended one so far (Point Reyes or Ohlone/Big Sur), this trip is mandatory. If this presents a conflict, email 50-miler2026@t222.org immediately.The ScheduleFriday, May 8th: Meet at the Troop 222 Shed immediately after school. We will depart absolutely no later than 4:30 PM. We will drive to Tahoe Valley Campground to establish base camp and begin acclimating.Dinner: Grab a bagged dinner or bring money for a stop along the way. Saturday, May 9th: Early wake-up (pre-sunrise). We drive to the Mt. Tallac Trailhead to beat the crowds and practice an efficient "alpine start."Sunday, May 10th: Breakfast, break camp, and return home (ETA: Afternoon-ish) to ensure everyone is rested for the school week.The Hike: Mt. Tallac (Desolation Wilderness)Distance: 10 miles round-trip, (5 miles OUT and 5 miles BACK).Elevation: Starting at 6,300 feet and ascending to nearly 10,000 feet.Difficulty: Strenuous. Expect a continuous, steep ascent.Resources: [AllTrails Link Here] [NOAA Weather Forecast Here] [Caltopo Map Here]Note on Safety: If we encounter significant snow, we will turn back. We are prioritizing safety over the summit, as we are not yet trained for specialized snow gear (microspikes/ice axes).Gear & PackingPack as if you are going on the actual 50-Miler. Even though we are at a campground, we are testing your full kit. [Packing List]Do not forget to attend the Pack Check this Thursday April 23rd (it will be required for the regular Troop Meeting after anyway!)The Scout Motto is "Be Prepared," bring EVERYTHING as if going on the outing that night!The "Insulation" Layer: Mandatory. Temps can drop into the 20s°F at night. Do NOT leave your layers at home!Water: You must have the capacity to carry 3-4 Liters. Water is scarce on this trail, and the extra weight provides vital training.Food: Leadership will prepare Saturday Breakfast, trail snacks, and lunch. Action ItemsRSVP: Update your status on TroopTrack ASAP.Saturday Dinner Volunteers: We’re having a "Victory Dinner" on Saturday (real food, not dehydrated!). If you want to help cook a meal for a Patrol, [Fill out this form]. Assignments will be confirmed 2 weeks prior. We will be in campground, so we can plan to bring car camping materials, should your meal plan require it. Physical Prep: If you haven't been hiking, start now. 10,000 feet is not the place to find out you need more cardio!Questions? Reach out to the leadership team at 50-miler2026@t222.org.

events.summary: Memorial Day Flag Planting at Golden Gate National Cemetery
events.location: JHM8+8Q, 1300 Sneath Ln, San Bruno, CA 94066
events.description: Memorial Day is coming up (later this month)! This day has come to signal the beginning of summer in the United States. But what many might not realize is that the holiday originated during the American Civil War when citizens placed flowers on the graves of those who had been killed in battle. Today, we celebrate Memorial Day as a time to honor all those who have died in the nation’s wars.If you are not attending the Angel Island campout, please consider spending a part of your morning on Saturday, May 23 with your fellow Scouts and join us in placing flags at Golden Gate National Cemetery to pay tribute to the men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice in defense of our country. As a scout, I've been to this event three times before, and every time it's a great way to start the day and remember that our freedom isn't free. The Golden Gate National Cemetery just 20 miles north of Menlo Park. It was first opened in 1941 and is the final resting place of almost 150,000 soldiers and sailors, as well as 15 Medal of Honor recipients, Congressman Leo Ryan, and Admiral Chester W. Nimitz, commander of the US Pacific Fleet during World War II.We will be part of Scouts, Active and Retired Military, War Veterans and other community groups from all over the Bay Area, gathering in tribute to these gallant men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice in defense of our country.Importantly, donuts, juice and coffee will be served Scouts are expected to wear class A uniform. PLEASE RSVP SO WE GET A GOOD HEAD COUNT.A logistics email will follow as we get closer to the event (we are simply waiting for those details from the Pacific Skyline Council).YIS,Miller Hartstein

UPDATES as of May 2026: One large consolidated "hybrid crew" format. SYT and additional safety regulations will be prioritized and strongly enforced. This large crew will go to Emigrant Wilderness (we were unable to secure sufficient permits for the original/other itinerary). We’ll be heading into the High Sierras of the Emigrant Wilderness for a 6-day backpacking adventure featuring alpine terrain, breathtaking views, and a premiere Scouting experience. This trek will take place from Monday July 27 and we will return no later than Sunday August 2.⛰️ Trip Itinerary:At a glance, this itinerary starts and ends at Kennedy Meadows Pack Station, builds steadily through classic granite-and-meadow Sierra terrain, and culminates with a high crossing over Big Sam before exiting through Kennedy Valley back to the trailhead.Read more about the route and day-to-day narrative by visiting these resources: [Big Sam Trip Summary and Notes][Big Sam Coordinates and Mileage]🥾 50-Miler Award OpportunityScouts (and adults!) who complete this trek can qualify for the BSA 50-Miler Award. They must also do at least 10 hours of conservation work (can be completed anytime before/during/after trek). Questions or concerns? Email 50-miler2026@t222.org
